ilike-case-insensitive.label: Case-Insensitive Matching
ilike-case-insensitive.summary: Perform case-insensitive searches with ILIKE or similar.

sk1.title: Understanding Case-Insensitive Matching in SQL
sk1.bodyMarkdown: When searching for text in SQL, you may want to ignore letter casing (upper or lower case). The `ILIKE` operator (in some SQL dialects) allows you to perform case-insensitive pattern matching, so 'apple', 'Apple', and 'APPLE' are all considered matches. In SQLite, you can use the `LIKE` operator with the `COLLATE NOCASE` clause to achieve similar results.

q1.title: Choosing the Right Operator
q1.prompt: Which SQL operator is commonly used for case-insensitive pattern matching in databases that support it?
q1.hint: Think about the operator that ignores letter casing.

help.concept: Case-insensitive matching allows you to search text regardless of upper or lower case. Some SQL dialects have a specific operator for this.
help.hint_1: It's similar to LIKE, but specifically for case-insensitive searches.
help.hint_2: The operator starts with an 'I' to indicate 'insensitive'.

q2.title: Case-Insensitive Search in SQLite
q2.prompt: Which of the following can be used to perform a case-insensitive search in SQLite? (Select all that apply)
q2.hint: Consider how SQLite handles case sensitivity with LIKE.

help.concept: SQLite's LIKE operator is case-insensitive for ASCII by default, but COLLATE NOCASE can be used for clarity. ILIKE is not supported in SQLite.
help.hint_1: One of these options is not supported in SQLite at all.
help.hint_2: Both LIKE (default) and LIKE with COLLATE NOCASE can be used for case-insensitive searches in SQLite.

q3.title: Fill in the Blank: Case-Insensitive Pattern
q3.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to find all products whose name contains 'phone', regardless of case, in SQLite:

SELECT * FROM products_catalog WHERE name _____ '%phone%';
q3.hint: Which operator is used for pattern matching in SQLite?

help.concept: In SQLite, the LIKE operator is used for pattern matching and is case-insensitive for ASCII characters.
help.hint_1: ILIKE is not available in SQLite.
help.hint_2: The operator you need is the standard one for pattern matching in SQLite.

q4.title: Write a Case-Insensitive Query
q4.prompt: Write an SQL query to select all rows from the products_catalog table where the name contains the word 'laptop', ignoring case.
q4.hint: Use LIKE and consider how to ignore case in SQLite.

help.concept: To perform a case-insensitive search in SQLite, use LIKE with COLLATE NOCASE.
help.hint_1: Add COLLATE NOCASE to your LIKE clause.
help.hint_2: Your WHERE clause should use LIKE with a pattern and COLLATE NOCASE.
